preregistration: this file + partition/SPEC.md, committed before any generation (record-first). Partition ruling is the orchestrator's pre-registered judgment (partition-spec.md, embedded verbatim in partition.py as SPEC_TEXT and written to partition/SPEC.md by that script) and is followed exactly, not re-made here.
design: 4-arm hermetic ep-partition: without / with-full / with-operational / with-conceptual. Treatment construction (07-09 pattern): stripped SKILL.md body + 3 touched references, filename-headered. with-full uses the working-tree source files verbatim; with-operational / with-conceptual substitute partition.py's OPERATIONAL / CONCEPTUAL variant of each file, same join. Tests which content partition of engineering-principles carries the measured d_new effect (07-09, +0.206 CI [+0.038,+0.373]): operational-only, conceptual-only, both, or neither.
executor: claude -p --model sonnet, CLAUDE_EFFORT=medium, empty external cwd (WSL-native default /tmp/claude-1000/hx_ep_partition_cwd, HERMETIC_CWD-overridable, checked empty of CLAUDE.md), tools denied (Skill Task Read Grep Glob Bash Edit Write WebFetch WebSearch NotebookEdit), user CLAUDE.md relocated on request (HERMETIC_RELOCATE_USER_MEMORY=1, trap-restored), prompt via stdin, --append-system-prompt = BASE_SYS + treatment for non-without arms
grading: reuses benchmarks/2026-07-08-skills-hermetic/{grade,prosecute_counts}.workflow.js (model sonnet), blind (arm withheld); met_final = min(grader_met, prosecutor_met) per ADR 0025. Grok cross-family re-grade lane run on identical blinded items as a divergence check (ADR-standing grok-as-agent-lane practice); a grok/claude verdict flip is recorded as exploratory, not a KEEP/CUT promotion on its own.

prescreen.rule: bare (without-equivalent) frac_met >= 0.85 on a committed prior run = saturated, drop before spending new generation on it; a committed 'without' arm from the same skill/eval set at the same evals.json content stands in for a fresh 1-rep prescreen (ADR 0076 spirit: don't re-measure what's already recorded).
prescreen.source: benchmarks/2026-07-09-ep-remeasure-hermetic/results.jsonl, 'without' column (bare arm, same 6 evals, same evals.json)
prescreen.result: E1 0.833, E2 0.800, E3 0.778, E4 0.600, E5 0.200, E6 0.200 -- no eval >= 0.85, none saturated/dropped. E1 (0.833) is flagged borderline (closest to the 0.85 ceiling) but kept: still below threshold and it was the eval with the smallest d_new headroom in 07-09 (+0.17), worth re-observing under the partition split. All 6 evals proceed to the grid (6 evals x 4 arms x 3 reps = 72 cells).

cost.estimate_note: per-cell cost derived from benchmarks/2026-07-17-code-standards-remeasure/metadata.json actuals (single-turn tool-denied text cells, same harness family): bare $1.45/6=$0.242 (prescreen) -> without $0.24; with $0.282 and $0.363 (2 pilot cells, mean $0.323) -> $0.32 applied uniformly to all 3 with-* arms (a claude -p single-turn text call's cost is dominated by fixed per-call + output-token overhead; the with-arms' input-token spread here, 2.2k-22.8k chars, is small next to that fixed cost, so a uniform with-arm estimate is the defensible read pending the pilot's actuals). 72 cells = 6 evals x 4 arms x 3 reps: generation ~= 18*$0.24 (without) + 54*$0.32 (3 with-arms) = $4.32 + $17.28 = $21.60. Grading (blind grader+prosecutor + grok cross-family lane) ~$4-9 per issue #244's own comparable-grid citation (bs-iter3 36-cell grid, $12.02 all-in). Total estimate ~$25-35 all-in (issue #244); ceiling_usd = 2x the upper estimate (ADR 0073).

actuals.note: filled after the pilot + grid run; ceiling and pilot_cap above are pre-registered stop rules, not optimizations (ADR 0076/0073)

partition.spec_source: partition-spec.md (orchestrator ruling, 2026-07-18), embedded verbatim in partition.py and written to partition/SPEC.md for the audit trail
partition.toc_policy: Table of Contents blocks dropped from both OPERATIONAL and CONCEPTUAL variants (navigation, not content); with-full (arm B) keeps them, verbatim 07-09 construction
partition.reconciliation_note: for each file, operational_chars + conceptual_chars + toc_dropped_chars exceeds raw_full_chars by a small joiner/heading-duplication overlap that partition.py prints per-file (SKILL.md +24, design-review.md +34, root-cause-analysis.md +21, ssot-enforcement.md +37): the H1 title is retained in BOTH variants (structural, not classified -- the spec never names it, see README ambiguity note) and, for the two files with a SPLIT Core Principle section (design-review.md, ssot-enforcement.md), the '## Core Principle' heading is likewise emitted once per variant. Summing that duplication (h1_chars + duplicated_heading_chars, from partition/chars.json) accounts for all but +2 chars on each SPLIT-section file; the residual +2 is '\n\n'.join() reconstruction whitespace (a fresh joiner appears where TOC removal or a sentence-level split changed local adjacency), not misclassified content -- every non-TOC content character is assigned to exactly one of {operational, conceptual} except the deliberately-duplicated H1/heading.
